paper_title: Population Genomic and Phylogenomic Analysis of Scrubwrens

methods.description: This method involves filtering SNP datasets and performing various statistical analyses to understand genetic diversity and structure in scrubwren populations.

steps.description: We first filtered our data and removed any non-neutral loci using BAYESCAN 2.1.
steps.searchable_summary: Filter SNP data to remove non-neutral loci using BAYESCAN 2.1

steps.description: For the filtered dataset, we estimated the level of missing data in PLINK 1.0.9.
steps.searchable_summary: Estimate missing data levels using PLINK 1.0.9

steps.description: Calculated summary statistics in GENODIVE 2.0b27 (effective number of alleles and observed heterozygosity) and CERVUS 3.0.7 (polymorphic information content).
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate genetic summary statistics using GENODIVE and CERVUS

steps.description: Estimated nucleotide diversity and Tajima's D in DnaSP 6 using the strict fasta file obtained from STACKS.
steps.searchable_summary: Estimate nucleotide diversity and Tajima's D using DnaSP 6

methods.description: This method involves generating and analyzing a ddRADseq dataset to confirm species identity and reconstruct phylogenomic relationships among scrubwrens.

steps.description: We sampled 74 scrubwrens across an elevational gradient in Mt Wilhelm, Papua New Guinea and generated a genome-wide ddRADseq dataset.
steps.searchable_summary: Generate ddRADseq dataset from scrubwren samples

steps.description: We obtained ~104 million cleaned reads from the ddRADseq runs.
Confirmed species identity based on both the COI gene network and phylogenomic tree.
steps.searchable_summary: Confirm species identity using COI gene network and phylogenomic tree

methods.description: This method describes the process of capturing scrubwrens, collecting blood samples, and extracting DNA for genomic analysis.

steps.description: We mist-netted birds along an elevational gradient on Mt. Wilhelm and collected blood from 74 captured scrubwren individuals.
steps.searchable_summary: Capture scrubwrens and collect blood samples

steps.description: Stored blood samples in 95% ethanol prior to DNA extraction.
Used the DNeasy Blood and Tissue Kit to extract genomic DNA following the manufacturer's instructions.
steps.searchable_summary: Extract genomic DNA using DNeasy Blood and Tissue Kit

steps.description: Quantified DNA using a Qubit 2.0 high sensitivity DNA Assay kit.

methods.method_name: ddRADseq Data Filtering and Data Matrix Generation
methods.description: This method involves filtering and processing ddRADseq data to generate data matrices for phylogenomic and population genomic analyses.

steps.description: Performed a visual quality check for the raw data using FASTQC v.0.11.5.
steps.searchable_summary: Perform quality check on raw data using FASTQC

steps.description: Filtered and demultiplexed raw reads using the process_radtags program within the STACKS 1.44 pipeline.
steps.searchable_summary: Filter and demultiplex reads using STACKS process_radtags

steps.description: Trimmed the reads to 140 bp and removed all reads that contained at least one uncalled base or were otherwise of low quality.
Used pyRAD 3.0.66 pipeline for phylogenomic analyses based on concatenated sequence data.
steps.searchable_summary: Process data for phylogenomic analysis using pyRAD

steps.description: Used the denovo_map.pl program within STACKS for SNP calling in population-genomic analyses.
